race_name: Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride

top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before January.
77 miles on paved coastal and hinterland roads burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride's paved coastal and hinterland roads rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride's paved coastal and hinterland roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 77 miles.
When someone attacks on paved coastal and hinterland roads, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride. Train it until it feels boring.
At Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,796ft of climbing on paved coastal and hinterland roads does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved coastal and hinterland roads. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Base fitness for Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Cadel Evans Great Ocean Road Ride’s 77 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Durability, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 77 miles of paved coastal and hinterland roads in Geelong, Victoria, Australia.
